confidence_framework.T1: Primary government source (Bihar government portals, BIADA, official policy documents, cabinet decisions)
confidence_framework.T2: Industry publication (Economic Times, Business Standard, ETGovernment, Press Trust of India)

executive_summary.policy_name: Bihar Industrial Investment Promotion Package 2025 (BIPPP-2025 / BIIPP-2025)
executive_summary.announced: August 26, 2025 (Cabinet decision chaired by CM Nitish Kumar)
executive_summary.validity: Applications must be submitted before March 31, 2026
executive_summary.objective: Boost industrial growth, create approximately 1 crore (10 million) jobs over 5 years, and position Bihar as a serious investment destination
executive_summary.predecessor: Bihar Industrial Investment Promotion Policy, 2016 (BIIPP-2016). BIIPP-2025 is described as a '2.0 version' building on the 2016 foundation.
executive_summary.biada_role: Bihar Industrial Area Development Authority (BIADA) is the nodal agency for land acquisition, industrial area development, land allotment, and shed management. It is implementing an ERP system to digitize land allotment and industrial processes.
executive_summary.fintech_city: FinTech City at Fatuha (Patna district) — 242 acres, ₹408.81 crore sanctioned for land acquisition. Modeled on Gujarat International Finance Tec-City (GIFT City). Located adjacent to Multi Modal Logistic Park (MMLP) at Fatuha circle.

free_land_tiers.land_allotment: Up to 10 acres free of cost
Up to 25 acres free of cost
Up to 10 acres free of cost

land_incentives.discounted_land: Other investors who do not meet the above criteria get land at 50% discount on BIADA land rates
land_incentives.land_bank: Approximately 15,000 acres already transferred or in process of acquisition for industrial use. Government approved acquisition of 14,600 acres for 32 industrial areas/parks in the past year. BIADA has acquired 8,000 acres since its inception.
land_incentives.district_coverage: In-principle decision to set up an industrial area or park in every district of Bihar
land_incentives.source: T1: Bihar Cabinet announcement (Aug 2025); T2: Economic Times, Business Standard, ETGovernment interviews

financial_incentives.sgst_reimbursement: Up to 300% of approved project cost for 14 years (for new units)
financial_incentives.capital_subsidy: Up to 30% of eligible capital investment
financial_incentives.export_incentive: Up to ₹40 lakh per year for 14 years (doubled from previous ₹20 lakh/year limit)

employment_incentives.textile_units: Up to ₹5,000 per month per employee + 300% of ESI and EPF
employment_incentives.other_units: Up to ₹2,000 per month per employee + 100% of ESI and EPF

financial_incentives.stamp_duty_reimbursement: Reimbursement of stamp duty and land conversion fees
financial_incentives.patent_registration: 50% reimbursement of expenses including attorney fees
financial_incentives.quality_certification: 50% reimbursement of costs for GMP, ISO, BIS, etc.
financial_incentives.source: T1: Bihar Cabinet announcement; T2: Economic Times, Business Standard, ETGovernment

eligibility_criteria.entity_types: Companies, LLPs, partnerships, proprietorships (specifically for industrial units)
eligibility_criteria.investment_threshold_for_land: ₹100 crore with 1,000 jobs (10 acres); ₹1,000 crore (25 acres); Fortune 500 (10 acres)
eligibility_criteria.minimum_investment_for_incentives: Not explicitly stated for all incentives, but the ₹100 crore + 1,000 jobs threshold is the primary tier for free land. Other investors may avail incentives at lower investment levels subject to policy terms.
eligibility_criteria.sector_focus: Textiles, apparel, food processing, agro-based industries, engineering goods, electronics assembly, IT/ITeS, MSMEs, renewable energy, and other manufacturing sectors
eligibility_criteria.source: T2: Economic Times, Business Standard, ETGovernment interviews

model_1_interest_subvention_plus_sgst.description: For SMEs and debt-heavy projects needing cheaper debt. Interest subvention combined with SGST reimbursement.
model_1_interest_subvention_plus_sgst.suitable_for: Small and medium enterprises struggling with cost of borrowing

model_2_sgst_reimbursement.description: For companies with high sales volume and high GST outflow. SGST reimbursement up to 300% of project cost over 14 years.

model_3_capital_subsidy.description: For capital-intensive industries requiring heavy machinery and infrastructure upfront. 30% capital subsidy.
model_3_capital_subsidy.suitable_for: Large manufacturing units with significant upfront investment

three_incentive_models.source: T2: ETGovernment interview with Additional Chief Secretary Mihir Kumar Singh (Sept 2025)

application_steps.step_1: Investor identifies project, investment size, and employment potential
application_steps.step_2: Choose incentive model (Interest Subvention + SGST / SGST Reimbursement / Capital Subsidy)
application_steps.step_3: Submit application through BIADA online portal (or designated government portal) before March 31, 2026
application_steps.step_4: BIADA / State Investment Promotion Board (SIPB) processes application with time-bound and transparent decision-making (virtual meetings available for investor observation)
application_steps.step_5: Upon approval, land allotment and incentive disbursal as per selected model
application_steps.note: Exact online portal URL and detailed form requirements are not fully specified in public sources. The BIADA portal (https://biada1.bihar.gov.in) has 'Apply Online (Land & Shed)' functionality.
application_steps.source: T2: ETGovernment, Business Standard; T1: BIADA portal

land_allotment_procedures.online_application: Available at BIADA portal: https://biada1.bihar.gov.in — 'Apply Online (Land & Shed)'
land_allotment_procedures.land_bank: BIADA maintains a 'Land Bank' showing available land and a 'Plug & Play Bank' for available sheds

process.step_1: Submit online application for land/shed through BIADA portal
process.step_2: BIADA evaluates application based on merit, investment, employment, innovation, and sector alignment
process.step_3: Payment of land cost (or confirmation of free land eligibility) and execution of lease/deed
process.step_4: Post-allotment, use eCollection Portal for payments
process.note: For free land under BIIPP-2025, the allotment is contingent on meeting investment and job thresholds. Investors must commit to creating the specified number of jobs.

policy_documents: BIADA Land Allotment & Management Policy, 2026 (As amended)
land_allotment_procedures.source: T1: BIADA portal (https://biada1.bihar.gov.in); T2: Economic Times, Business Standard

export_obligations.biipp_2025_export_incentive: Up to ₹40 lakh per year for 14 years for export promotion. This is an incentive, not an obligation.
export_obligations.sez_export_obligations: If the project is designated as a Special Economic Zone (SEZ) under the central SEZ Act, 2005, then the developer/unit must meet the following:
export_obligations.sez_net_foreign_exchange_earning: SEZ units must achieve positive Net Foreign Exchange Earning (NFE) within 5 years of commencement of production. NFE = Foreign Exchange earned minus Foreign Exchange spent (excluding capital goods and raw material imports).
export_obligations.sez_penalty_for_non_compliance: If a unit fails to achieve positive NFE within 5 years, it may lose SEZ status and benefits, and may be required to pay duties/taxes on imported goods as if they were imported into the Domestic Tariff Area (DTA). The exact penalty is governed by the SEZ Rules, 2006 and Development Commissioner discretion.
export_obligations.sez_developer_obligations: SEZ developers must complete infrastructure within specified timelines (typically 10 years for multi-product SEZs, 5 years for sector-specific SEZs) and maintain the SEZ as a fenced area with customs control.
export_obligations.note: BIIPP-2025 itself does not explicitly impose export obligations. However, if the industrial unit is in an SEZ, central SEZ laws apply. Bihar's BIIPP-2025 export incentive is a subsidy to encourage exports, not a legal requirement.
export_obligations.source: T2: Economic Times, Business Standard; T1: SEZ Act, 2005 (general knowledge)

biada_role.establishment: Established under the Bihar Industrial Area Development Authority Act, 1974
functions: Land acquisition and creation of land bank for industrial use
Development of industrial areas, parks, and zones
Land allotment and shed allotment to industrial units
Maintenance of industrial infrastructure (roads, water, power, drainage)
Implementation of industrial policies (BIIPP-2025, BIADA Amnesty Policy, Exit Policy)
ERP implementation (in progress) for digitizing land allotment, HR, finance, project management, and investment promotion

current_initiatives.erp_project: BIADA is implementing an end-to-end ERP solution for Project Management System (PMS), land allotment, service delivery, investment promotion, finance, HR, and vendor management. Completion target: 32 weeks from award.
current_initiatives.land_acquisition: 8,000 acres acquired since inception; 14,600 acres approved for 32 industrial areas/parks in the past year; 15,000 acres in pipeline
current_initiatives.industrial_parks_every_district: In-principle decision to have an industrial area/park in every district

biada_role.contact: Executive Director (Operations), BIADA, Udyog Bhawan, East Gandhi Maidan, Patna 800004. Phone: 0612-2675898
biada_role.source: T1: BIADA portal (https://biada1.bihar.gov.in); T2: Economic Times, Business Standard

existing_sezs_in_bihar.note: Bihar does not currently have a large, fully operational multi-product SEZ under the central SEZ Act, 2005. The state has been focusing on 'Special Industrial Zones' (SIZs) and dedicated industrial parks rather than formal SEZs. However, the following related zones exist or are planned:

planned_zones.name: FinTech City at Fatuha
planned_zones.location: Patna district, adjacent to MMLP at Fatuha circle
planned_zones.model: Modeled on Gujarat International Finance Tec-City (GIFT City)
planned_zones.status: Land acquisition phase; cabinet sanctioned in August 2025
planned_zones.source: T1: Bihar Cabinet; T2: Business Standard, Economic Times

planned_zones.name: Special Industrial Zones (SIZs) across districts
planned_zones.sectors: Toys and plastics (Begusarai, West Champaran); Pharmaceuticals (Hajipur); Textiles; Food processing; Renewable energy
planned_zones.status: Planning phase; new industrial policy at final stage

planned_zones.name: Bihar Integrated Manufacturing City Gaya Limited (BIMCGL)
planned_zones.status: Referenced on BIADA portal; detailed status not available in public sources

existing_sezs_in_bihar.sez_gap: There is no confirmed, fully operational large SEZ in Bihar as of early 2026. The state is pursuing 'Special Industrial Zones' under the new industrial policy rather than formal SEZ Act status. Investors seeking SEZ benefits (duty-free imports, single-window clearance, tax holidays) may need to apply for SEZ designation under the central SEZ Act, 2005, which is separate from BIIPP-2025.

fintech_city_fatuha.name: FinTech City at Fatuha
fintech_city_fatuha.model: Modeled on Gujarat International Finance Tec-City (GIFT City) located between Ahmedabad and Gandhinagar
fintech_city_fatuha.adjacent_infrastructure: Multi Modal Logistic Park (MMLP) at Fatuha circle
fintech_city_fatuha.status: Land acquisition phase; cabinet approved in August 2025
fintech_city_fatuha.relation_to_sez: Not explicitly designated as an SEZ under the SEZ Act, 2005 in current announcements. It is a dedicated zone for FinTech and digital services. If SEZ status is sought, a separate application to the Ministry of Commerce & Industry (SEZ Board of Approval) would be required. The 242-acre size is smaller than typical multi-product SEZs (which require 1,000+ hectares or 2,500+ acres), but sector-specific SEZs can be smaller (10+ hectares for sector-specific with 100% FDI).
fintech_city_fatuha.source: T1: Bihar Cabinet announcement (Aug 2025); T2: Business Standard, Economic Times

gaps.gap: Whether Bihar has any formally notified SEZ under the SEZ Act, 2005, and if so, their exact status, export performance, and NFE achievement
gaps.action: Query Ministry of Commerce & Industry SEZ portal (sezindia.nic.in) or contact Bihar Industries Department for SEZ status list

gaps.gap: Exact application form, URL, and document checklist for BIIPP-2025 incentives — not fully detailed in public sources
gaps.action: Contact BIADA or Bihar Industries Department for the official BIIPP-2025 application form and process manual

gaps.gap: Whether FinTech City at Fatuha will apply for SEZ status under the SEZ Act, 2005, or remain a state-level industrial zone
gaps.action: Contact Bihar Industries Secretary or Infrastructure Development Authority Patna for SEZ intent

gaps.gap: Detailed BIADA Land Allotment & Management Policy, 2026 text — exact allotment rates, lease terms, transferability, and exit conditions
gaps.action: Download BIADA Land Allotment & Management Policy 2026 from https://biada1.bihar.gov.in/ActRule.aspx

gaps.gap: Current land allotment rates per acre at each BIADA cluster (Patna, Bihta, Gaya, Hajipur, Muzaffarpur, etc.)
gaps.action: Check BIADA 'Land Bank' and 'eCollection Portal' for current rates

gaps.gap: Whether BIIPP-2025 incentives can be combined with central SEZ benefits (duty drawback, STPI, EOU status) or if they are mutually exclusive
gaps.action: Review BIIPP-2025 fine print and seek clarification from Bihar Industries Department and MoCI SEZ Division

gaps.gap: Status of Bihar Integrated Manufacturing City Gaya Limited (BIMCGL) — whether it is operational, land allotted, or still in planning
gaps.action: Contact BIADA or Bihar Industries Department for BIMCGL status report

gaps.gap: Detailed breakdown of the ₹408.81 crore Fatuha FinTech City budget — how much for land acquisition vs. infrastructure development
gaps.action: Request Bihar Cabinet note or Infrastructure Development Authority budget document
